message:
refactor(bd-3ewx): Extract daemon commands to daemon.rs module

Phase 3 of bd-3ewx: Split commands.rs into modular command files.

Extracted daemon lifecycle commands:
- daemon_status, daemon_start, daemon_stop
- daemon_restart, daemon_reload, daemon_logs
- which_rchd helper function

Changes:
- New rch/src/commands/daemon.rs (754 lines)
- Updated mod.rs with re-exports for backward compatibility
- Made send_daemon_command pub(crate) for submodule access
- Reduced mod.rs from 11,511 to 10,773 lines (-738 lines)

All 63 tests pass, clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1mzg","title":"Test: Hook Classification Timing Budget (<1ms/<5ms)","description":"## Purpose
Test that hook classification decisions meet the strict timing budgets documented in AGENTS.md.

## Timing Budgets (from AGENTS.md)
- Non-compilation rejection: <1ms, panic at 5ms
- Compilation acceptance: <5ms, panic at 10ms

## Test Cases

### Non-Compilation Commands (<1ms)
1. Simple commands:
   - `ls`, `echo hello`, `pwd`
   - Measure: time from input to "allow local" decision
   - Assert: < 1ms for 99th percentile

2. Git commands:
   - `git status`, `git diff`, `git log`
   - Assert: < 1ms (NEVER_INTERCEPT fast path)

3. Docker commands:
   - `docker ps`, `docker build`
   - Assert: < 1ms (NEVER_INTERCEPT fast path)

### Compilation Commands (<5ms)
4. Cargo commands:
   - `cargo build`, `cargo test`, `cargo check`
   - Measure: time to classify and decide to offload
   - Assert: < 5ms for 99th percentile

5. C compiler commands:
   - `gcc`, `g++`, `clang`
   - Assert: < 5ms classification

6. Complex commands:
   - Long command strings with many arguments
   - Assert: still < 5ms

### Statistical Validation
7. Run 1000 iterations:
   - Calculate mean, p50, p95, p99
   - Assert: p99 < budget
   - Assert: variance is low (stable performance)

## Measurement Method
```rust
let start = Instant::now();
let decision = classify_command(cmd);
let elapsed = start.elapsed();
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Non-compilation: p99 < 1ms
- Compilation: p99 < 5ms
- No single measurement exceeds panic threshold
- Low variance across runs

## MANDATORY Logging

All tests MUST use TestLogger with structured JSON output:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_classification_timing","phase":"setup","msg":"Preparing benchmark","data":{"iterations":1000,"warmup":100}}
{"ts":"...","level":"DEBUG","test":"test_classification_timing","phase":"measure","msg":"Single measurement","data":{"cmd":"ls","decision":"allow_local","elapsed_us":47}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_classification_timing","phase":"stats","msg":"Non-compilation stats","data":{"cmd_type":"simple","n":1000,"mean_us":52,"p50_us":48,"p95_us":89,"p99_us":156,"max_us":312}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_classification_timing","phase":"stats","msg":"Compilation stats","data":{"cmd_type":"cargo","n":1000,"mean_us":1847,"p50_us":1623,"p95_us":3412,"p99_us":4156,"max_us":5892}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_classification_timing","phase":"verify","msg":"Timing budget check","data":{"category":"non_compilation","p99_us":156,"budget_us":1000,"within_budget":true}}
{"ts":"...","level":"WARN","test":"test_classification_timing","phase":"verify","msg":"Approaching panic threshold","data":{"category":"compilation","max_us":9500,"panic_threshold_us":10000}}
```

### Required Logging Points
1. Benchmark setup (iterations, warmup count)
2. Individual measurements (DEBUG level for each iteration)
3. Statistical summary per command category (mean, p50, p95, p99, max)
4. Budget verification (within/exceeds budget)
5. Warning when approaching panic thresholds
6. Clear PASS/FAIL verdict with all percentile data","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:26:48.056493826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:30:49.411612805Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:30:49.411336083Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-1nhd (Performance & Timing Budget Tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mzg","depends_on_id":"bd-17tg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:27:03.805578424Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mzg","depends_on_id":"bd-1nhd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:27:01.972845620Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1nhd","title":"True E2E Performance & Timing Budget Tests","description":"# Feature: True E2E Performance & Timing Budget Tests

## Purpose

Test that rch meets its strict performance budgets documented in AGENTS.md to ensure it doesn't slow down AI agent workflows.

## MANDATORY Logging Requirements

Performance tests MUST use structured JSON logging with precise timing:

### Performance-Specific Log Events

```json
// Timing measurement
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_hook_classification_ls","phase":"measure","msg":"Classification timing","data":{"cmd":"ls -la","classification":"non_compile","tier":2,"elapsed_us":234,"budget_us":1000,"within_budget":true}}

// Budget check
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_hook_classification_ls","phase":"verify","msg":"Budget verification","data":{"metric":"hook_decision","measured_us":234,"budget_us":1000,"panic_threshold_us":5000,"status":"pass"}}

// Statistical summary
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_hook_classification","phase":"stats","msg":"Timing statistics","data":{"samples":100,"mean_us":245,"p50_us":230,"p95_us":380,"p99_us":520,"max_us":890,"stddev_us":87}}

// Full pipeline overhead
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_pipeline_overhead","phase":"measure","msg":"Pipeline timing","data":{"local_ms":5432,"remote_ms":5890,"overhead_ms":458,"overhead_pct":8.4,"budget_pct":15,"status":"pass"}}

// Panic threshold alert
{"ts":"...","level":"WARN","test":"test_hook_panic","phase":"verify","msg":"Approaching panic threshold","data":{"measured_us":4200,"panic_threshold_us":5000,"headroom_pct":16}}
```

### Timing Metrics to Log

1. **Microsecond precision**: for sub-millisecond budgets
2. **Statistical summary**: mean, p50, p95, p99, max, stddev
3. **Budget comparison**: measured vs budget vs panic threshold
4. **Overhead calculation**: local baseline, remote total, overhead %
5. **Sample count**: number of measurements for statistical validity

## Why This Matters

- EVERY Bash command passes through the rch hook
- Slow hook = slow agent = frustrated users
- AGENTS.md specifies hard timing budgets:
  - Hook decision (non-compilation): <1ms, panic at 5ms
  - Hook decision (compilation): <5ms, panic at 10ms
  - Worker selection: <10ms, panic at 50ms
  - Full pipeline: <15% overhead, panic at 50%

## Performance Scenarios to Test

### Hook Classification Performance

1. **Non-compilation command rejection (<1ms)**
   - `ls -la` - Log: classification time, tier, decision
   - `git status` - Log: must not hit compilation path
   - `echo hello` - Log: shell builtin handling
   
2. **Compilation command acceptance (<5ms)**
   - `cargo build` - Log: tier 4 classification time
   - `cargo test` - Log: argument parsing time
   - `gcc hello.c` - Log: C compiler detection time
   
3. **Complex command classification**
   - Long command strings - Log: scaling with length
   - Commands with many arguments - Log: arg parse time
   - Piped commands - Log: should be rejected quickly

### Worker Selection Performance

1. **Single worker selection (<10ms)**
   - Log: selection algorithm time, worker chosen
   
2. **Multi-worker selection with scoring**
   - Log: scoring time per worker, total time
   
3. **Selection with cache affinity matching**
   - Log: cache lookup time, affinity bonus
   
4. **Selection under heavy load** (all slots busy)
   - Log: queue time, slot wait if any

### Full Pipeline Overhead

1. **Small project (< 1000 files)**: overhead < 15%
   - Log: file count, sync time, compile time, total overhead
   
2. **Medium project (< 10000 files)**: overhead < 15%
   - Log: sync scaling, compile scaling
   
3. **Large project (> 50000 files)**: overhead < 15%
   - Log: detailed phase timing breakdown
   
4. **Incremental builds (cached)**: minimal overhead
   - Log: cache hit rate, reduced sync time

### Streaming Performance

1. **Output streaming latency**
   - Log: first byte time, steady state throughput
   
2. **Large output handling (>1MB)**
   - Log: buffering behavior, memory usage
   
3. **Progress indicator responsiveness**
   - Log: update frequency, display latency

## Test Methodology

```rust
pub struct TimingTest {
    samples: Vec<Duration>,
    budget: Duration,
    panic_threshold: Duration,
    logger: TestLogger,  // MANDATORY
}

impl TimingTest {
    /// Run timing test N times, log each measurement
    pub fn measure_n(&mut self, n: usize, f: impl Fn()) {
        for i in 0..n {
            let start = Instant::now();
            f();
            let elapsed = start.elapsed();
            self.samples.push(elapsed);
            self.logger.timing(i, elapsed, self.budget);
        }
    }
    
    /// Calculate and log statistics
    pub fn summarize(&mut self) -> TimingStats {
        let stats = compute_stats(&self.samples);
        self.logger.stats(stats, self.budget, self.panic_threshold);
        stats
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] ALL timing budgets met under normal conditions
- [ ] Panic thresholds never exceeded
- [ ] Performance stable across multiple runs (low variance)
- [ ] No regression from baseline measurements
- [ ] ALL tests emit structured JSON logs with timing
- [ ] Statistical summaries logged for every performance test
- [ ] Measurements include p95/p99 percentiles

## Technical Notes

- Use std::time::Instant for precise timing
- Run tests multiple times (N >= 100) for statistical significance
- Test on both cold and warm caches
- Document baseline measurements for comparison
- Log system info (CPU, load average) for context

## Dependencies

- Requires bd-3saj (test infrastructure for timing measurement)
- Requires bd-12hi (cargo compilation for full pipeline tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"HazySparrow","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:21:35.417435322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:31:54.183463187Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:31:54.183390281Z","close_reason":"Completed true-e2e performance & timing budget tests (hook decision + selection + consistency)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nhd","depends_on_id":"bd-12hi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nhd","depends_on_id":"bd-1cwg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nhd","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:27Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-1nhd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete: Created tests/true_e2e/performance_tests.rs with 6 tests covering timing budgets. Tests verify non-compilation (<1ms), compilation (<5ms), complex commands, worker selection, accuracy, and consistency. All tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-26T23:10:00Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1nk5","title":"EPIC: Error Handling and User Messaging","description":"# Error Handling and User Messaging

## Overview
RCH has an excellent error infrastructure using miette for rich diagnostics. However, not all error paths use it consistently, leading to user-unfriendly error messages in some cases.

## Current State

### Strong Points
- Proper error types in rch/src/error.rs with miette Diagnostic
- Error codes follow RCH-Exxx convention
- Rich help text with remediation suggestions

### Problems Found
There are ~35 `bail\!()` calls in commands.rs that bypass the structured error system:

```rust
// Line 2167 - No error code, no context, no remediation
bail\!("rch-wkr not found on remote");

// Line 2197 - Same problem  
bail\!("rustup install failed: {}", stderr.trim());

// Line 2943 - Generic error
bail\!("rustup install failed: {}", stderr.trim());
```

These plain string errors:
1. Have no error code for scripting/automation
2. Provide no remediation steps
3. Don't indicate which worker/host failed
4. Can't be programmatically parsed

## Error Code Gaps
Current codes have gaps suggesting incomplete coverage:
- E001-E004: Config (has E005-E006 gap)
- E007-E009: Worker 
- E100-E108: SSH (has gaps)
- E202, E204, E207: Worker health (missing E203, E205, E206)
- No E300+ category defined for build errors

## Key Files
- rch/src/error.rs - Error type definitions
- rch/src/commands.rs - Where most bail\!() calls live
- rch-common/src/ui/error.rs - ErrorPanel display

## Success Criteria
- Every user-facing error has an RCH-Exxx code
- Every error includes actionable remediation steps
- Error codes are documented in a public reference
- No bare bail\!() calls without structured context","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:58:56.426351214Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T16:54:44.401981745Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1nmv","title":"Add configurable external timeout for all remote commands to prevent zombie processes","description":"# Feature: Configurable External Timeout Protection

## Background

Per user request, extend the bun test timeout protection to ALL remote commands to prevent zombie/stuck/misbehaving processes from bringing down worker machines.

## Current State

- bd-15ma added hardcoded 600s timeout for BunTest/BunTypecheck commands
- Other command types (cargo test, cargo build, gcc, etc.) have no external timeout protection
- SSH command_timeout exists but may not work for CPU-bound stuck processes

## Proposed Solution

1. Add configurable timeout settings to CompilationConfig:
   - `default_external_timeout_sec: u64` (default: 3600 = 1 hour)
   - `test_external_timeout_sec: u64` (default: 1800 = 30 minutes)
   - `bun_external_timeout_sec: u64` (default: 600 = 10 minutes, per known issues)

2. Modify `wrap_with_external_timeout()` to:
   - Apply to ALL commands, not just bun
   - Use appropriate timeout based on CompilationKind
   - Add config option to disable (`external_timeout_enabled: bool`)

3. Add monitoring/logging:
   - Log when timeout kills a process (exit code 137 = SIGKILL)
   - Track timeout events in telemetry

## Benefits

- Prevents runaway processes from consuming worker slots indefinitely
- Protects against various stuck scenarios (infinite loops, deadlocks, resource contention)
- Configurable per command type for flexibility","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:28:26.248490139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T20:46:07.910883617Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T20:46:07.909259266Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3dv2","title":"Epic: rich_rust Integration - Premium Terminal Output for RCH","description":"# Epic: rich_rust Integration - Premium Terminal Output for RCH

## Executive Summary

This epic encompasses the complete integration of the rich_rust library into RCH (Remote Compilation Helper) to provide beautiful, premium terminal output for human operators watching AI coding agents work. The integration must maintain **zero interference** with AI agents, which are the primary users of RCH.

## Background & Motivation

### The Problem
RCH currently outputs plain text for status, errors, and progress. While functional, this provides a poor experience for humans monitoring AI agents. When operators run 15+ agents simultaneously, they need at-a-glance visual feedback about:
- Which workers are healthy/degraded/offline
- What compilations are in progress
- Where errors occurred and how to fix them
- Transfer and benchmark progress

### The Solution
Integrate rich_rust (a Rust port of Python's Rich library) to provide:
- Styled text with markup syntax ([bold red]text[/])
- Tables with auto-sizing columns and Unicode borders
- Panels for framed content (errors, status)
- Progress bars for transfers and benchmarks
- Color-coded status indicators
- Automatic terminal capability detection and color downgrade

## The Cardinal Rule: Agent-First Architecture

**CRITICAL**: AI coding agents are the PRIMARY users of RCH. They interact through:
1. JSON hook protocol (stdin -> stdout)
2. Exit codes (0, 1, 101, 128+N)
3. Streamed compilation output

Rich formatting must NEVER interfere with these machine interfaces. The design principle is:
- **stderr for humans**: Rich output goes to stderr
- **stdout for machines**: Structured data stays pristine on stdout
- **Graceful degradation**: If rich_rust fails, fall back to plain text

## Output Classification

### NEVER Add Rich Formatting (Machine Output)
- Hook JSON responses (stdout)
- --json flag output (stdout)
- Compilation output passthrough (stdout)
- Test output streaming (stdout)
- Exit codes (process exit status)

### ADD Rich Formatting (Human Output)
- rch status commands (stderr)
- rch workers list/probe/benchmark (stderr)
- rchd startup banner and health (stderr)
- Error messages and warnings (stderr)
- Transfer and benchmark progress (stderr)

## Implementation Phases

1. **Phase 1: Foundation** - Infrastructure, modules, context detection
2. **Phase 2: CLI Commands** - Rich output for status/worker commands
3. **Phase 3: Daemon UI** - Startup banner, health displays
4. **Phase 4: Error Experience** - Beautiful error panels
5. **Phase 5: Progress & Feedback** - Progress bars, spinners
6. **Phase 6: Polish** - Theme refinement, docs, validation

## Success Criteria

1. All rch CLI commands have beautiful, informative output
2. AI agents function identically with rich output enabled/disabled
3. NO_COLOR and FORCE_COLOR environment variables are respected
4. Performance: Zero overhead on hook classification hot path
5. All existing tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:00:06.187551979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T07:08:31.061597056Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T07:08:31.061493863Z","close_reason":"EPIC COMPLETE: rich_rust Integration - Premium Terminal Output for RCH. All 6 phases closed: Phase 1 (Foundation), Phase 2 (CLI Commands), Phase 3 (Daemon UI), Phase 4 (Error Experience), Phase 5 (Progress & Feedback), Phase 6 (Polish & Documentation). RCH now has beautiful, context-aware terminal output that works correctly with both humans and AI agents.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3eaa","title":"Worker Preflight Health Guards (Load + Disk)","description":"# Worker Preflight Health Guards (Load Average + Disk Space)

## Merged Scope
This bead consolidates two related worker preflight check features:
- **Load Average Guard** (formerly bd-2hbf): Skip workers with high CPU load
- **Disk Space Guard** (formerly bd-1mij): Skip workers with low free disk

Both are preflight checks that improve worker selection quality by filtering out unhealthy workers before job assignment.

## Background
Even if slots are available, a worker may be unsuitable:
- High load average spikes hurt build times
- Low disk space can cause builds to fail or slow dramatically

Preflight guards prevent wasted transfers and improve overall compilation success rates.

## Goals
1. Skip workers whose load average exceeds a threshold (per-core normalized)
2. Skip workers whose free disk space is below minimum
3. Provide clear selection reasons (NoWorkersWithCapacity, NoWorkersWithDisk)
4. Fail-open: if all workers exceed thresholds, pick best available with warning

## Design / Approach

### Configuration
```toml
[selection]
max_load_per_core = 2.0    # Skip if load1 / cores > threshold
min_free_gb = 10.0         # Skip if free disk < threshold
```

### Telemetry Fields
Extend worker capabilities/telemetry:
- `load_avg_1`, `load_avg_5`, `load_avg_15` (from /proc/loadavg or uptime)
- `disk_free_gb`, `disk_total_gb` (from df or statfs)

### Selection Logic
1. Filter by slot availability
2. Filter by load-per-core threshold
3. Filter by disk space threshold
4. Sort by SpeedScore
5. If all filtered out, pick best available with warning

## Tasks / Subtasks
1. Extend telemetry payload to include load avg and disk metrics
2. Add fallback SSH probe (uptime, df) when telemetry unavailable
3. Add config fields with validation
4. Update selection logic with reason codes
5. Expose in `rch status` and `rch workers list` output
6. Add warnings when using degraded workers

## Tests

### Unit Tests
- `rchd/src/selection.rs`: load-per-core threshold logic, disk space filter
- `rchd/src/telemetry.rs`: load avg parsing, disk metric extraction
- Selection reason codes for each guard type

### Integration Tests
- Mock worker reports high load -> skipped
- Mock worker reports low disk -> skipped
- All workers degraded -> pick best with warning

### E2E Tests
Script: `scripts/e2e_preflight_guards.sh`

Scenarios:
- Mixed load -> skip high, select low
- Mixed disk -> skip low-disk, select adequate
- All high load -> select least-loaded with warning
- All low disk -> select most-available with warning
- Status output displays load and disk warnings

## Acceptance Criteria
- High-load workers are skipped automatically
- Low-disk workers are skipped automatically
- Selection reasons are visible and actionable
- Thresholds are configurable and documented
- Fail-open behavior preserves system usability

## Logging & E2E
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, duration_ms, result, error
- Phases: classify, select (with guard reasons), sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary

## Edge Cases & UX
- Missing telemetry -> allow worker but warn
- Use load-per-core to normalize across heterogeneous machines
- Support per-worker threshold overrides
- Selection reason includes specific guard signals

## Dependencies
- Depends on bd-155i (Worker capabilities report + mismatch warnings)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"WhiteLake","created_at":"2026-01-26T00:30:20.175037620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:55:28.801894925Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:55:28.801827188Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3eaa","depends_on_id":"bd-155i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:28Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-3eaa","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Progress - Phase 1 Complete

### Changes Made

1. **WorkerCapabilities extended** (rch-common/src/types.rs)
   - Added health metrics: `num_cpus`, `load_avg_1`, `load_avg_5`, `load_avg_15`
   - Added disk metrics: `disk_free_gb`, `disk_total_gb`
   - Added helper methods: `load_per_core()`, `is_high_load()`, `is_low_disk()`

2. **SelectionConfig extended** (rch-common/src/types.rs)
   - Added `max_load_per_core: Option<f64>` (default: 2.0)
   - Added `min_free_gb: Option<f64>` (default: 10.0)

3. **Worker probing** (rch-wkr/src/main.rs)
   - Added `probe_num_cpus()` - nproc (Linux) or sysctl (macOS)
   - Added `probe_load_average()` - /proc/loadavg or uptime
   - Added `probe_disk_space()` - df -P -k /tmp

4. **Selection logic** (rchd/src/selection.rs)
   - Added preflight filtering in `get_eligible_workers()`
   - Workers exceeding thresholds are skipped with debug logging
   - Workers kept for fail-open fallback

5. **Unit tests** (rch-common/src/types.rs)
   - test_load_per_core_calculation
   - test_is_high_load
   - test_is_low_disk
   - test_selection_config_preflight_defaults
   - test_selection_config_preflight_serde
   - test_worker_capabilities_health_metrics_serde

### All Tests Passing
- rch-common: 723 tests
- rchd: 23 tests
- All crates compile

### Remaining
- CLI output: Show load/disk info in `rch workers list` (optional)
- E2E test script","created_at":"2026-01-27T05:55:17Z"}]}

## Target Files
- rch-common/src/remote_compilation.rs - Remote compilation logic
- rch-common/src/toolchain.rs - Toolchain detection

## Existing Test Files (need expansion)
- rch-common/tests/ - Has integration tests but gaps remain

## Test Requirements
1. **Remote Compilation Tests**
   - Test command translation
   - Test argument handling
   - Test path mapping
   - Test error wrapping

2. **Toolchain Detection Tests**
   - Test rustc version parsing
   - Test cargo detection
   - Test gcc/clang detection
   - Test bun/node detection
   - Test missing toolchain handling

## Constraints
- NO MockFileSystem for path operations
- Use real file system with temp dirs
- Test actual toolchain detection
- Skip tests if toolchain not installed (graceful)

## Logging Requirements
- Each test: TEST START/TEST PASS format
- Log detected toolchain versions
- Include PATH and env on detection failures","notes":"Verified 680 tests (675 unit + 5 integration) in rch-common. -{"id":"bd-3ewx","title":"Split commands.rs into modular command files","description":"# Split commands.rs into Modules

## Problem
rch/src/commands.rs is 10,432 lines - too large to navigate, review, or maintain effectively.

## Current Structure
```
commands.rs
├── Response types (lines 93-582, ~490 lines)
├── Helper functions (lines 150-250, 396-416, ~120 lines)
└── Command implementations (lines 600-10432, ~9800 lines)
    ├── Config commands (~800 lines)
    ├── Daemon commands (~600 lines)
    ├── Workers commands (~2000 lines)
    ├── Fleet commands (~1000 lines)
    ├── Hook commands (~500 lines)
    ├── Queue/Cancel commands (~400 lines)
    ├── Self-test commands (~800 lines)
    ├── Update commands (~500 lines)
    ├── Agents commands (~300 lines)
    └── Misc helpers (~2900 lines)
```

## Proposed Structure
```
rch/src/commands/
├── mod.rs              # Re-exports, shared imports
├── types.rs            # Response structs (WorkerInfo, etc.)
├── helpers.rs          # Shared utilities
├── daemon.rs           # start, stop, restart, status, logs
├── workers.rs          # list, probe, drain, enable, benchmark, etc.
├── config.rs           # show, init, validate, set, lint, etc.
├── fleet.rs            # deploy, drain, status, upgrade
├── hook.rs             # install, uninstall, test
├── queue.rs            # queue, cancel
├── self_test.rs        # self-test command
├── update.rs           # update command
└── agents.rs           # agents commands
```

## Migration Strategy

### Phase 1: Extract Types (no behavior change)
1. Create commands/types.rs
2. Move all response structs
3. Update imports in commands.rs
4. Verify tests pass

### Phase 2: Extract Helpers
1. Create commands/helpers.rs
2. Move shared functions
3. Make public what's needed
4. Verify tests pass

### Phase 3: Extract Command Groups (one at a time)
1. Create commands/daemon.rs
2. Move daemon command handlers
3. Re-export from mod.rs
4. Verify tests pass
5. Repeat for each command group

## Public API Preservation
The extraction must not change the public API:
- Same function signatures
- Same error types
- Same command behavior
- Same test coverage

## Files to Create
- rch/src/commands/mod.rs
- rch/src/commands/types.rs
- rch/src/commands/helpers.rs
- rch/src/commands/daemon.rs
- rch/src/commands/workers.rs
- rch/src/commands/config.rs
- rch/src/commands/fleet.rs
- rch/src/commands/hook.rs
- rch/src/commands/queue.rs
- rch/src/commands/self_test.rs
- rch/src/commands/update.rs
- rch/src/commands/agents.rs

## Files to Delete (after migration)
- rch/src/commands.rs (replaced by commands/mod.rs)

## Testing
- All existing tests must pass
- No new warnings from clippy
- Build time should not increase significantly","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:11.313521582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:18:40.259423793Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","depends_on_id":"bd-lym6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:45:34Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":99,"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
Each extracted module needs its own test file:
1. `rch/src/commands/types_test.rs` - All type serde roundtrips
2. `rch/src/commands/helpers_test.rs` - All helper functions
3. `rch/src/commands/daemon_test.rs` - Daemon command handlers
4. `rch/src/commands/workers_test.rs` - Worker command handlers
5. `rch/src/commands/config_test.rs` - Config command handlers

### Integration Tests
1. All existing tests must continue passing
2. No new clippy warnings
3. Module imports work correctly

### Regression Test Script (scripts/test_commands_refactor.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE=${RCH_TEST_LOG:-/tmp/rch_refactor_test.log}

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

log "Testing commands.rs refactor"

# Test 1: All tests pass
log "Test 1: cargo test"
cargo test -p rch 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# Test 2: No clippy warnings
log "Test 2: cargo clippy"
cargo clippy -p rch --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# Test 3: No unused imports/dead code
log "Test 3: Check for dead code"
cargo check -p rch 2>&1 | grep -i "unused\|dead_code" && {
  log "WARN: Unused code detected"
}

# Test 4: API unchanged
log "Test 4: Verify public API unchanged"
cargo doc -p rch --no-deps 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

# Test 5: Module file sizes
log "Test 5: Verify no file exceeds 2000 lines"
for f in rch/src/commands/*.rs; do
  lines=$(wc -l < "$f")
  if [ "$lines" -gt 2000 ]; then
    log "FAIL: $f has $lines lines (max 2000)"
    exit 1
  fi
  log "OK: $f has $lines lines"
done

log "Refactor tests complete"
```

### Logging Requirements
- Document module structure in code comments
- Log module load times at TRACE level (for build analysis)","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:58:20Z"},{"id":104,"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","author":"DarkMeadow","text":"Phase 2 helpers extraction progress by DarkMeadow: added default_socket_path to helpers.rs, removed 5 duplicate SSH helpers from mod.rs, updated imports, fixed test module. mod.rs -139 lines. Tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-01-30T22:18:40Z"},{"id":100,"issue_id":"bd-3ewx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Assessment: mod.rs is 11,303 lines. Phase 1 (types.rs) partial - 560 lines extracted. Identified helper functions for Phase 2: default_socket_path, runtime_label, extract_version_numbers, major_version, rust_version_mismatch, ssh_key_path, classify_ssh_error, format_ssh_report, indent_lines, humanize_duration, urlencoding_encode. This is a multi-session refactoring effort - recommend incremental extraction starting with helpers.","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:27:40Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3fda","title":"Standardize TUI status indicators using StatusIndicator enum","description":"# Standardize TUI Status Indicators

## Problem
The TUI uses multiple icon sources inconsistently:

### In widgets.rs lines 154-160 (header status):
```rust
let status_text = match state.daemon.status {
    Status::Running => "● Running",
    Status::Stopped => "○ Stopped",
    Status::Error => "✗ Error",
    Status::Unknown => "? Unknown",
};
```

### In widgets.rs lines 237-242 (worker status):
```rust
let status_icon = match w.status {
    WorkerStatus::Healthy => "●",
    WorkerStatus::Degraded => "◐",
    WorkerStatus::Unreachable => "○",
    WorkerStatus::Draining => "◑",
};
```

### In CLI commands (using StatusIndicator):
```rust
StatusIndicator::Success.display(style)  // Returns themed ✓
StatusIndicator::Error.display(style)    // Returns themed ✗
```

## Impact
- Inconsistent appearance between TUI and CLI
- TUI doesn't respect theme settings
- Hardcoded symbols don't adapt to terminal capabilities
- Accessibility features in StatusIndicator not used

## Solution
1. Import StatusIndicator into TUI widgets
2. Create TUI-specific display method that returns &str for ratatui
3. Replace all hardcoded symbols with StatusIndicator calls
4. Ensure colors are sourced from ColorScheme

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/tui/widgets.rs
- rch/src/ui/theme.rs (may need TUI-specific display method)

## Testing
- Verify TUI matches CLI appearance
- Test with --high-contrast mode
- Test with color-blind palettes
- Verify symbol consistency","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:03:16.913248958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:33:33.504121012Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:33:33.504057734Z","close_reason":"Standardized TUI status indicators using StatusIndicator enum and Symbols struct. Replaced all hardcoded symbols in widgets.rs (daemon header, worker status, active builds, build history) with centralized Symbols::UNICODE and StatusIndicator-based lookups. Added indicator_color helper for ratatui Color bridging. All symbols now respect theme/accessibility settings. 5 new integration tests. All 1719 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fda","depends_on_id":"bd-lcoa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3fda","depends_on_id":"bd-ryot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:45:50Z","created_by":"import"}]}
